faqList.question: Do I need to have a CV to use PATHEW?
faqList.answer: No! PATHEW is designed for everyone, whether you’re starting from scratch or updating an existing CV. You can build a professional CV from the ground up using our guided onboarding form and step-by-step prompts.

faqList.question: Is PATHEW free to use?
faqList.answer: PATHEW offers a free plan that gives you access to free credits to build functional CVs. Once you top up your credit, you’ll be able to create more CVs, cover letters, and grant proposals.

faqList.question: Can I download my CV in different formats?
faqList.answer: Yes. Once your CV is complete, you can download it in PDF, Word (.docx), or plain text format, making it easy to submit to any employer or job platform.

faqList.question: How long does it take to create a CV with PATHEW?
faqList.answer: Most users complete a polished, professional CV in under 15 minutes. If you already have your work history and key details ready, it can take even less time.

faqList.question: Can I create multiple CVs for different job applications?
faqList.answer: Absolutely. PATHEW allows you to save and manage multiple CV versions, so you can tailor each one to a specific role or industry without starting over each time.

faqList.question: Are the CV templates suitable for my industry?
faqList.answer: PATHEW offers a wide range of CVs designed for various industries and career levels, from creative fields to corporate, tech, healthcare, and more. You can prompt PATHEW to develop a CV for an industry that suits you best.

faqList.question: Is my personal data safe on PATHEW?
faqList.answer: Yes. PATHEW takes data privacy seriously. Your information is encrypted, never shared with third parties, and you remain in full control of your data at all times.

step2.title: Assistant Analysis

step3.title: Direct Application

story.p1: PATHEW started as a small internal tool used by international researchers to find funding. We quickly realized that the biggest barrier to global success wasn't talent—it was access to information and the ability to package that talent effectively.
story.p2: Today, we empower thousands of professionals across 120+ countries to navigate the complex landscape of fellowships, grants, and high-impact roles with ease and precision.

team.title: The 3-Man Core
team.subtitle: Meet the visionaries behind the PATHEW Assistant engine.
team.desc1: The visionary behind PATHEW, dedicated to breaking down geographical barriers for talent worldwide.
team.desc2: Product strategist focused on building intuitive Assistant tools that empower professional growth.
team.desc3: Strategic lead for global expansion, ensuring the PATHEW Assistant reaches every corner of the globe.

sections.title: 1. Introduction
sections.p: This Privacy Policy describes how Extraordinary Woman Blog, Pathew (the "Platform"), a service of A-aston Technologies Limited (the "Company", "we", "us", "our"), collects, uses, and protects personal information. The platform is powered by Pathew AI assistant

sections.title: 2. Who we are;
sections.p: A-aston Technologies Limited (registered office 124-128 City Road, London, England, EC1V 2NX) is the controller for the personal data processed through Extraordinary Woman Blog (the "Platform"). Contact: privacy@extraordinarywomanblog.com

sections.title: 3. Scope
sections.p: This Policy covers personal data processed via the Platform, including our CV Builder, Cover Letter/Proposal Builder, Grant Builder, and Opportunity Matching features, all powered by our AI assistant, Pathew.

sections.title: 4. Data we collect
sections.p:
Account & contact data: name, email, country, preferences. Profile data: year of birth, education, skills, work history, and other information you provide to build your CV, cover letter, proposal, or grant application.
Usage data: device/browser information, IP address, and event logs related to your use of the Platform.
Job-search materials: CVs/résumés, cover letters, job descriptions, role preferences, prompts
Marketing preferences: brand-level opt-ins, unsubscribes.

sections.title: 5. How we use data
sections.p:
Provide services and improve the Platform: to build, edit, and generate your CV, cover letter, proposal, or grant application.
Opportunity matching: we use your profile information, including year of birth, solely to match you with opportunities that specify year of birth or age as a criterion. We do not use your year of birth for any other purpose.
Communicate with you: to send administrative messages relevant to your use of the Platform.

sections.title: 6. AI processing
sections.p: Our platform is powered by an AI API. When you use Pathew to generate content (such as a CV or cover letter), we send the minimum necessary information to API providers to generate your response. Your data and responses are not used to train the model of our provider or any other AI model.

sections.title: 7. Sharing Personal Data
sections.p: We do not sell or rent your personal data. We may share personal data with service providers who support our operations, such as hosting providers, analytics providers, and email delivery services, partners who act on our instructions and are bound by appropriate data protection terms. We otherwise do not share your personal data with third parties, except where required by law or to protect the rights, safety, or security of our users or the Platform.

sections.title: 8. Cookies & similar technologies
sections.p:
We use cookies and similar technologies to run the Platform and improve your experience. These fall into a few categories:
Essential cookies: necessary to operate the Platform, such as keeping you signed in and maintaining your session.
Preference cookies: remember choices you make, such as language or display settings.
Analytics cookies: help us understand how the Platform is used so that we can improve it.
You can manage your preferences through our cookie banner or your browser settings. Please note that disabling certain cookies may affect how parts of the Platform function.

sections.title: 9. Marketing Purposes
sections.p: We only send marketing communications to users who have opted in. You may withdraw consent at any time by using the unsubscribe link in any marketing message, or by emailing privacy@extraordinarywomanblog.com.

sections.title: 10. Data retention
sections.p: We retain your account and profile data for as long as your account remains active, or as needed to provide services in the Platform. You may request deletion of your data at any time. We also follow UK GDPR policies regarding data deletion and consent.

sections.title: 11. Your rights
sections.p: You may request access, correction, deletion, restriction, or portability of your personal data, and withdraw consent at any time, by emailing privacy@extraordinarywomanblog.com. You may also lodge a complaint with your local data protection authority. In the United Kingdom, this is the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO).

sections.title: 12. Security
sections.p: We implement technical and organisational measures, including access controls and encryption, to protect your personal data. We continuously review and update these measures in line with industry standards and evolving security practices. We also encourage users to safeguard their own account credentials and report any suspected unauthorized access promptly.

sections.title: 13. Children
sections.p: The Platform is not directed at children under 13. If you believe a child has provided us with personal data, please contact privacy@extraordinarywomanblog.com so we can remove it.

sections.title: 14. Changes to this Policy
sections.p: We may update this Policy from time to time. Changes will be posted here with a new "Last updated" date.

sections.title: 15. Contact us
sections.p:
A-aston Technologies Limited
124-128 City Road London, England, EC1V 2NX (Proprietary letters only, please email instead)
Email: privacy@extraordinarywomanblog.com

sections.title: 1. Who we are
sections.p:
In these Terms, "Extraordinary Woman Blog/Pathew (Platform", "the Platform", "we", "our" and "us" ) refer to A-aston Technologies Limited, a company registered in England and Wales, with its registered office at 124-128 City Road, London, England, EC1V 2NX.
General enquiries: hello@extraordinarywomanblog.com
Data enquiries: privacy@extraordinarywomanblog.com

sections.title: 2. The Services these Terms cover
3. The words we use
sections.p:
Content: any text, graphics, or other material found within the Services, including AI Output.
User Content: anything you upload or give us, such as your CV, work history, education details, and prompts.
Paid Services: features you access through a paid subscription, which provides credits used to generate content within the Platform.
Credits: the units allocated to your account on subscription, which you spend to generate CVs, cover letters, proposals, grant applications, or other Platform output.
AI Output: any CV, cover letter, proposal, or grant application that Pathew generates for you.

sections.title: 4. Agreeing to these Terms
sections.p: When you use any part of the Services, you are agreeing both to these Terms and to our Privacy Policy. If either of them does not sit well with you, please do not use the Services. Carrying on with them tells us you accept both.

sections.title: 5. Eligibility & accounts
sections.p: To use the Services, you must be old enough to give valid digital consent where you live. If you are under 18, a parent or guardian must agree to these Terms on your behalf. We do not knowingly accept data from children under 13 (U.S.) or under 16 (UK/EU). When you register, the details you give us should be true and current, and looking after your login credentials is your responsibility. Anything done through your account is treated as done by you, so please tell us straight away if you notice activity you did not authorise.

sections.title: 6. How you may and may not use the Platform
sections.p:
You must not: infringe anyone's intellectual property or privacy rights; harass others or promote hateful or illegal content; introduce malware; scrape data at scale; reverse engineer the Services; or misrepresent your identity, qualifications, or eligibility in any CV, proposal, or grant application you generate or submit.
Fair use
To keep the Platform stable and reliable for everyone, we may queue, limit, or temporarily restrict certain requests or features at our discretion.

sections.title: 7. Privacy
sections.p: Our Privacy Policy sets out how we collect, use, and protect your personal data. It covers how your year of birth is used only for opportunity matching, and how AI processing through the our API works. The Privacy Policy forms part of these Terms.

sections.title: 8. Your content; feedback
sections.p:
You grant us a worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free licence to host, store, process, and adapt your User Content, solely so we can run, secure, and improve the Services. Every other right in your content stays with you. If you send us feedback, you give us a perpetual, irrevocable licence to use it freely.
Confidentiality
Each of us will keep the other's non-public information confidential and use it only to provide or receive the Services. This does not cover information that is already public, developed independently, or obtained lawfully from someone else. We may disclose information where the law requires it.

sections.title: 9. Our intellectual property
sections.p: Everything within the Services apart from your User Content belongs to us or to those who license it to us, and is protected by intellectual property law. You may not copy, change, distribute, or build derivative works from our Content without first getting our written permission.

sections.title: 10. AI Output and accuracy
sections.p: Pathew, powered by an API, produces CVs, cover letters, proposals, and grant applications from the information you give it. AI Output can be wrong, incomplete, or out of date. We provide it for general information only; it is not professional career, legal, or grant-writing advice. Before you send any AI Output to an employer, funder, or other third party, the responsibility for checking that it is accurate and suitable rests with you.

sections.title: 11. Pricing, credits, renewals & refunds
sections.p:
All prices include VAT unless otherwise stated. The Platform runs on credits: when you subscribe, your account is allocated a set number of credits, which you spend each time you generate content such as a CV, cover letter, proposal, or grant application.
Your subscription, and the credits that come with it, does not renew automatically at the price then in effect. Users are to top up their credit when it’s exhausted.
When you purchase credits, you expressly request immediate access to the services associated with those credits. If you are a consumer in the UK or another jurisdiction that provides a statutory cooling-off period, you acknowledge that once credits have been added to your account and access to the services has begun, your right to cancel may be reduced or lost to the extent permitted by applicable law.
Credits are generally non-refundable once they have been used. Unused credits may be refunded only where required by law or where we determine, at our discretion, that exceptional circumstances apply, such as a technical fault that prevented the services from being provided.
We reserve the right to suspend or terminate access to the Platform where payment is reversed, disputed, fraudulent, or otherwise remains unpaid.
Any refund approved by us will be returned to the original payment method where possible or otherwise stated and agreed by the customer and us.

Talk to us first. Reach out to support and give us a fair chance to put things right before asking for your money back.
Something's broken. If a genuine, repeatable fault stops you from using your credits as intended, we may refund you. Sharing the details that let us see the problem for ourselves screenshots, error text, the steps that triggered it helps us act on it.
Bought by mistake. If you tell us within 30 minutes of being charged that a purchase was accidental, and you have not spent any meaningful number of credits in that window, we may refund it.
On renewal. We generally do not refund a subscription once it has renewed, unless the law requires it or one of the fault situations above applies.

sections.title: 12. Communications & marketing
sections.p: We may send you service-related and transactional messages, such as account notices and billing confirmations, without separate consent, since these are part of providing the Services. Any marketing communications, however, only go to people who have opted in. You can unsubscribe from marketing whenever you like, using the link in any marketing email or your account settings.

sections.title: 13. Other platforms we connect to
sections.p: The Services may link to third-party platforms such as job boards, grant portals, and application systems. We do not control their content, availability, or practices, so using them is at your own risk and subject to their own terms.

sections.title: 14. Security & beta features
sections.p: We put technical and organisational measures in place, including access controls and encryption, to protect the Services, and we keep reviewing and updating them. From time to time we may also offer beta or experimental features on an as-is basis, and these may change or be withdrawn at any time.

sections.title: 15. No guarantees of outcome
sections.p: We provide software tools to help you prepare applications. We are not an employment agency, a recruitment business, or a grant-making body, and we do not guarantee interviews, offers, funding, or any particular result.

sections.title: 16. Warranties; “as is”
sections.p: We offer the Services in the state they are in and as they happen to be available at any given moment. So far as the law allows, we make no promises about them whether spelled out, implied, or arising by statute including any assurance that they are fit for a particular purpose, free of errors, or suited to what you have in mind. You take them as you find them.

sections.title: 17. Limitation of liability
sections.p:
Some kinds of loss sit outside what we will cover: knock-on or indirect losses, and things like lost profits, lost data, or business you miss out on, including anything traceable to an error in AI Output. This holds whether a claim is framed as breach of contract, negligence, or anything else.
None of this touches the things the law says we cannot sign away, such as liability for death or personal injury resulting from negligence.

sections.title: 18. Indemnity
sections.p: You agree to defend us and cover our losses and costs (including reasonable legal fees) where they arise from: your use of the Services; your User Content or any AI Output you choose to rely on or submit; your breach of these Terms or of the law; or any misrepresentation in an application you submit.

sections.title: 19. Events beyond our control
sections.p: Neither of us is responsible for delays or failures caused by things outside our reasonable control, such as outages, cyberattacks, government action, or changes made by third-party platforms.

sections.title: 20. Suspension & termination
sections.p: We may suspend or end your access at any time for breach of these Terms, risk, or non-payment, and you may delete your account whenever you wish. Any provisions that are meant to outlast the agreement including Sections 8 to 10 and 16 to 24 will continue to apply.

sections.title: 21. Changes to the Services
sections.p: We may change, pause, or withdraw any part of the Services. If we significantly cut back the core functionality of a Paid Service, we will give you at least 30 days' notice and offer a pro-rata refund of prepaid fees if you cancel before the change takes effect.

sections.title: 22. Transferring these Terms
sections.p: We may pass these Terms on to an affiliate or a successor. You may not transfer them to anyone else without our written permission.

sections.title: 23. Governing law & jurisdiction
sections.p: English law governs these Terms. The courts of England and Wales have exclusive jurisdiction, subject to any mandatory consumer rights that let you bring proceedings in your local courts.

sections.title: 24. The agreement as a whole
sections.p: These Terms and our Privacy Policy make up the entire agreement between us. If any part of them turns out to be unenforceable, the rest stays in force. Headings are there for convenience and do not affect how the Terms are read.

sections.title: 25. Notices & contact
sections.p: We may send notices to the email address linked to your account. If you have questions about these Terms, contact us at privacy@extraordinarywomanblog.com.
